diagnosis.primary_leak: The model treated Phoenix's 43-point first half as sufficiently repeatable without discounting the unstable scoring created by Connecticut turnovers and transition opportunities.
secondary_leaks: First-half points off turnovers and fast-break scoring were not regressed aggressively enough.
A likely second-half possession decline with Phoenix protecting a double-digit lead was underweighted.
Phoenix's weak perimeter profile was not penalized enough; the Mercury were 2-of-9 from three at halftime and finished 0-of-6 after halftime.
The projection distribution was too narrow around the mean and understated left-tail scoring risk.
The quoted edge barely cleared the minimum threshold and should not have qualified as a full $10 strike after fragility adjustments.
what_worked: The market identification was coherent: Phoenix had 43 at halftime and needed only 39 in the second half.
Phoenix continued generating paint and free-throw opportunities, scoring 18 paint points and attempting 14 free throws after halftime.
The stake was capped at $10 and no correlated add-on was made.
what_failed: Phoenix scored only 11 points in the third quarter on 4-of-17 shooting.
The Mercury went 0-of-6 from three in the second half.
Estimated second-half pace fell by roughly 8 percent from the first-half rate.
Second-half offensive efficiency dropped from approximately 105.5 to 77.5 points per 100 possessions.

required_patches.name: Unstable Scoring Discount
required_patches.rule: At halftime, discount points generated from opponent turnovers and fast breaks toward team baseline before projecting a team total. Apply the larger discount when those categories account for at least 30 percent of first-half scoring.

required_patches.name: Lead-State Pace Compression
required_patches.rule: For teams leading by 8 or more at halftime, apply a second-half possession reduction unless the opponent projects to maintain comeback pressure and efficient scoring.

required_patches.name: Perimeter Fragility Penalty
required_patches.rule: Reduce over projections when a team has low three-point volume or efficiency and the over depends on sustained paint conversion above baseline.

required_patches.name: Left-Tail Expansion
required_patches.rule: Increase team-total variance when the offense is dependent on transition, turnovers, or free throws rather than stable half-court shot quality.

required_patches.name: Full-Stake Edge Gate
required_patches.rule: A full live stake requires at least a 6 percentage-point edge after all fragility and game-state adjustments. Edges of 4 to 5.9 points are half-stake only; below 4 points is a pass.

process_verdict.future_action: downgrade similar halftime team-total overs to pass or half-stake until LF-006 through LF-010 are implemented and backtested
